channel.title: McAmner Journal
channel.description: Films, books, music, objects, systems, and culture by Mattias Camner.

item.title: Le Samouraï
item.description: Le Samouraï is a note on Jean-Pierre Melville's 1967 film — solitude, ritual, the professional who has made silence into a system.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Zippo Lighter
item.description: Zippo Lighter is a note on the mechanism from Bradford, Pennsylvania — unchanged since 1932, still repairable, still windproof by design.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Victorinox Farmer
item.description: Victorinox Farmer is a note on the Swiss Army knife built around actual work — saw, blade, reamer, nothing decorative.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Stiletto Knife
item.description: Stiletto Knife is a note on Italian geometry, spring-loaded — the blade as the complete argument, nothing else needed.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Sony TC-D5M
item.description: Sony TC-D5M is a note on the professional portable cassette recorder — metal construction, VU meters, tape, and no abstraction between the operator and the sound.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: MacBook Air M4
item.description: MacBook Air M4 is a note on the machine that stopped being a compromise — silent, thin, and capable enough to stay.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Lamy 2000
item.description: Lamy 2000 is a note on the pen designed in 1966 that decided what a pen should be for the next fifty years.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso
item.description: Jaeger-LeCoultre Reverso is a note on the 1931 watch designed around a constraint — the case that flips to protect itself.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Custom Fender Stratocaster
item.description: Custom Fender Stratocaster is a note on a working instrument built around feel — the most copied design in electric guitar history, built right the first time.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Drake's Donkey Chore
item.description: Drake's Donkey Chore is a note on the British workwear jacket that looks worse new and better old — material that accumulates rather than degrades.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Chevrolet Camaro 1967
item.description: Chevrolet Camaro 1967 is a note on the first-generation pony car — long hood, short deck, steel drawn before it was decorated.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Arkangel
item.description: Arkangel is a note on James Rollins, Sigma Force, myth, archives, Arctic pressure, and the machinery of the global thriller.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: First Blood
item.description: First Blood is a note on David Morrell's 1972 novel, survival, trauma, authority, pursuit, and violence after war.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: the machine room
item.description: A note on local computation, owned infrastructure, and the philosophy of keeping real work on your own hardware.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Brave New World
item.description: Brave New World is a note on Aldous Huxley's 1932 novel, comfort, conditioning, pleasure, stability, and the quiet violence of a frictionless world.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Good Time
item.description: Good Time is a note on the Safdie brothers' 2017 film, panic, brotherhood, neon, bad decisions, and momentum without escape.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: coolThing
item.description: coolThing is a note on a repository for retro web experiments, local tools, Mega Movie, Mega Guitar, Mega Now, and browser interfaces with backend weight.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Unknown Pleasures
item.description: Unknown Pleasures is a note on Joy Division's 1979 album, post-punk, isolation, minimalism, and a room that will not let you leave.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Kill 'Em All
item.description: Kill 'Em All is a note on Metallica's 1983 debut, speed, energy, thrash, and the moment before polish.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner

item.title: Crime
item.description: Crime is a note on Ferdinand von Schirach's short cases, law, justice, morality, and complete worlds in miniature.
item.dc:creator: Mattias Camner
